venues.name: 7AM Bakers Umalas
venues.bestFor: early risers wanting breakfast/brunch before other cafés open; laptop/remote workers on strong wifi; sourdough and pastry lovers
venues.whatToOrder: sourdough; croissants and pastries; avocado toast; paninis/sandwiches; fresh cold-pressed juices
venues.whyItsHere: French loft-style artisan bakery and café in Umalas, opening early at 7am when most neighbouring cafés are still shut. Two-storey space known for sourdough, pastries and fresh coffee, with strong wifi and outdoor seating.
venues.notFor: late-night diners (kitchen is daytime-oriented); anyone wanting beachfront or nightlife

venues.name: Atlas Beach Club
venues.bestFor: A big lively day out or event with a group; sunset drinks in a large party setting
venues.whyItsHere: A vast beach-club-and-super-club complex on Berawa Beach spread across roughly 2.9 hectares, billed as one of the world's largest, with multiple pools, dining venues, bars, daily cultural performances and world-class entertainment and events. The spectacle-scale, all-day-into-night end of Canggu.
venues.notFor: Guests wanting a small, quiet or intimate venue

venues.name: BAKED Pererenan
venues.bestFor: A morning brunch after the beach, a pastry-and-coffee stop, and casual laptop-friendly daytime sitting.
venues.whatToOrder: House sourdough and pastries; specialty coffee
venues.whyItsHere: The Pererenan outpost of BAKED, an artisanal bakehouse and specialty-coffee brand, set in a converted garage with an open-air terrace and a modern brunch menu built on house sourdough and pastries.
venues.notFor: A late-night dinner or a formal sit-down occasion.

venues.name: Bali Buda Canggu
venues.bestFor: A wholesome, health-minded brunch or lunch; families; a calm cafe stop with groceries to grab on the way out.
venues.whyItsHere: The Canggu branch of Bali Buda, an organic wholefoods cafe-and-store brand established in 1994 — smoothie bowls, fresh juices, wholesome mains and an in-house bakery, with organic groceries and eco goods to take away.

venues.name: Bar Vera Bistro & Wine Bar
venues.bestFor: A stylish, relaxed date night, a late dinner or a wine-and-cocktail-focused evening with friends; couples and small groups who want European bistro plates with serious wine and drinks.
venues.whatToOrder: European wines by the glass and the cocktail/martini menu; barramundi with lime gelee; prawn risotto; charcoal-roasted chicken; banoffee souffle
venues.whyItsHere: A new-generation European bistro and wine bar in Pererenan (about 65 seats, inside a boutique hotel) pairing timeless French technique and locally sourced produce with a diverse European wine list and an award-winning cocktail programme, in a warm terracotta-and-wood room open daily until midnight.
venues.notFor: An evening dinner-and-drinks venue rather than a daytime coffee or work cafe.

venues.name: Boheme Canggu
venues.bestFor: A slow brunch, a laptop-and-coffee day by the pool, and easy all-day eating.
venues.whyItsHere: An all-day kitchen at the Shore Amora hotel in Pererenan serving fresh Western food with a healthy, locally-sourced twist, with a pool, daybeds and a nomad work space on site.
venues.notFor: A high-energy nightlife or beachfront-view outing.

venues.name: Brunch Club Pererenan
venues.bestFor: A lively all-day brunch or lunch with friends, couples or families; a relaxed sit-down for big sweet and savoury plates plus a cocktail.
venues.whatToOrder: Souffle Porncakes; eggs Benedict; French toast; smashed avo
venues.whyItsHere: An all-day, family-friendly brunch cafe in Pererenan set under a large mango tree, with an open, breezy, high-ceilinged space serving big brunch plates, lunch and cocktails. Best known for its fluffy souffle 'Porncakes'.
venues.notFor: A brunch-and-lunch venue rather than a late-night dinner spot.

venues.name: CAFE VIDA Healthy Organic Restaurant Canggu
venues.bestFor: Health-conscious eaters and vegans wanting an organic breakfast, lunch or dinner; a calm, wholesome brunch stop.
venues.whatToOrder: Shakshuka; raw gluten-free cakes; turmeric jamu
venues.whyItsHere: A dedicated healthy organic cafe and espresso bar on Batu Bolong, sourcing local permaculture-grown produce, with no palm oil, cane sugar, wheat flour or MSG and plenty of vegan and raw options.
venues.notFor: Diners looking for classic indulgent comfort food or big meat-heavy plates.

venues.name: COMO Beach Club Canggu
venues.bestFor: A polished sunset dinner or special occasion by the ocean; a leisurely Sunday brunch
venues.whyItsHere: The upscale beachfront club and restaurant at COMO Uma Canggu on Echo Beach, pairing a large ocean-facing pool with sunset dining, live acoustic music and DJ sets. Known for a refined design, seafood and COMO Shambhala wellness cuisine, plus a popular weekly beachfront Sunday brunch.

venues.name: Copenhagen CAFE Berawa
venues.bestFor: Daytime brunch and coffee, and shareable small-plate breakfasts in a photogenic setting.
venues.whatToOrder: The shared Nordic feast board of small dishes
venues.whyItsHere: A Scandinavian-inspired cafe serving an everyday "Nordic feast" - a build-your-own board of small dishes ticked off a menu card. The Berawa branch is the larger, calmer of its locations, open through the day.
venues.notFor: Not an evening dinner venue - daytime hours.

venues.name: Crate Cafe
venues.bestFor: Backpackers and surfers wanting a lively, affordable brunch after a morning in the water; people who like buzz and don't mind a crowd.
venues.whatToOrder: Smoothie bowls; all-day breakfast plates
venues.whyItsHere: A high-energy, industrial-style all-day breakfast institution on Jl. Batu Bolong, known for big smoothie bowls and a rotating menu chalked on the wall behind the counter.
venues.notFor: Anyone after a quiet, calm sit-down meal or a spot for focused laptop work.

venues.name: Hippie Fish Pererenan Beach
venues.bestFor: Beachfront sunset dinner and drinks; date night with a view; groups sharing seafood.
venues.whatToOrder: Grilled octopus; tuna crudo; king prawn tortellini.
venues.whyItsHere: A three-storey Mediterranean seafood restaurant and rooftop bar right on Pererenan Beach, with ocean views and a menu built around fresh fish. Casual cafe downstairs, air-conditioned dining above and an open-air rooftop for sunset.

venues.name: HOME by Chef Wayan
venues.bestFor: a considered balinese dinner; introducing someone to indonesian flavours done well; a table mixing meat, seafood and vegan diners
venues.fullAddress: Jl. Pantai Pererenan No. 92, Pererenan, Kec. Mengwi, Kabupaten Badung, Bali 80351
venues.whatToOrder: bebek betutu; babi guling; ayam srosop; braised lettuce with tempe crumble
venues.whyItsHere: Modern Balinese cooking from Chef Wayan Kresna Yasa in a seaside Pererenan setting, reworking family and island recipes with contemporary technique. The kitchen is spice- and produce-driven and keeps a full vegan section alongside its seafood and traditional dishes.
venues.notFor: travellers wanting a cheap warung price point; anyone after generic western comfort food

venues.name: ISLA by Earth Island
venues.bestFor: Surfers refuelling after a session, casual daytime meals, and anyone who wants a relaxed community-hub cafe rather than a formal restaurant.
venues.whatToOrder: Smoothie bowls, surfer platters, fresh coconuts
venues.whyItsHere: The all-day cafe inside Earth Island's surf compound on the Canggu shortcut: simple island food, coconuts and good coffee built around a surfer-and-creative community, with music, art and film events layered on top.
venues.notFor: A dressed-up dinner or a quiet fine-dining evening.

venues.name: La Brisa Bali
venues.bestFor: Sunset drinks and a laid-back beach day with friends; a relaxed shared meal by the water
venues.whatToOrder: Oysters from the raw/oyster bar
venues.whyItsHere: A rustic, sustainability-minded beach club on the foreshore of Echo Beach, hand-built from reclaimed wood salvaged from old Indonesian fishing boats, with a saltwater pool, a raw/oyster bar and wood-fired Mediterranean food. Best known for its driftwood aesthetic and long, open sunset views over the surf break.
venues.notFor: Guests wanting a polished high-rise pool-party scene rather than a rustic barefoot setting

venues.name: Luma
venues.bestFor: Golden-hour drinks and small plates; date night; a lively pre-dinner stop that becomes the evening.
venues.whatToOrder: Rustic Spanish- and Italian-style small plates for sharing.
venues.whyItsHere: An intimate, 30-seat open-air spot on Batu Bolong doing rustic Mediterranean small plates — Spanish- and Italian-style grazing — from chefs Cameron Emirali (10 Greek Street, London) and Kieran Morland (Merah Putih, Sangsaka). Draught beer, cocktails on tap and vinyl DJs on weekends.

venues.name: Mason
venues.bestFor: Date night; a special dinner; groups who want to share grilled plates and cured meats over cocktails.
venues.whyItsHere: A moody, minimalist grill on Batu Bolong (trading as MASONRY.) built around wood-fired, Mediterranean-leaning cooking, in-house charcuterie and a serious wine and cocktail list. It's one of Canggu's go-to rooms for a proper sit-down dinner.

venues.name: MIEL Specialty Coffee Canggu
venues.bestFor: Coffee-focused solo travellers and remote workers who want a proper filter coffee and a calm table to sit and work for a few hours; also an easy morning brunch.
venues.whatToOrder: Filter/brew-bar coffee (V60 or pour-over) and their signature single-origin filters; Big Brekkie; French toast with vanilla poached pears
venues.whyItsHere: A serious specialty-coffee cafe on Batu Bolong with a dedicated brew bar (V60, pour-over, cold brew) built on quality beans, set in a bright, plant-filled, high-ceilinged space. Spacious tables, quiet room and fast wifi make it a genuine work spot as much as a coffee stop.
venues.notFor: Best in the daytime rather than as an evening or late-night hangout.

venues.name: Milk & Madu Beach Road
venues.bestFor: Families with young children and groups; brunch through to an easy early dinner, plus daily sunset sessions.
venues.whatToOrder: Ricotta hotcakes; smashed avocado; lava-stone pizzas
venues.whyItsHere: A spacious, palm-shaded all-day cafe on Batu Bolong beach road with a kids' play area, brunch classics and lava-stone pizzas, part of the well-known Milk & Madu group.
venues.notFor: Couples seeking an intimate, quiet dining atmosphere.

venues.name: Milk & Madu Berawa
venues.bestFor: Families and groups; a big post-surf brunch; an easy all-day meal that suits fussy and hungry tables alike.
venues.whatToOrder: Ricotta hotcakes; lava-stone pizzas.
venues.whyItsHere: The Berawa flagship of Milk & Madu, a long-running all-day Canggu cafe brand doing hearty brunch, lava-stone pizzas and family-friendly dinners. A dependable, crowd-pleasing all-rounder.

venues.name: Milu by Nook
venues.bestFor: A relaxed brunch or dinner with rice-field views - couples and families wanting a green, calm setting.
venues.whyItsHere: A long-standing Berawa restaurant set in a garden overlooking a small rice paddy on Jl. Pantai Berawa, serving Balinese-Western dishes and coffee from morning until night.

venues.name: Moana Fish Eatery
venues.bestFor: Anyone wanting fresh, casual seafood for lunch or dinner without a formal setting.
venues.whatToOrder: Poke bowls and tuna carpaccio
venues.whyItsHere: A Tahitian-Polynesian fish eatery on Jl. Pantai Batu Bolong serving fresh seafood — poke bowls, sashimi, carpaccio, tartare and BBQ fish — in a laid-back setting, open from morning until late.
venues.notFor: Not ideal for red-meat eaters or non-seafood diners.

venues.name: Mosto Berawa
venues.bestFor: A date or intimate dinner centred on natural wine; a small group of wine lovers
venues.whatToOrder: Natural wine from the 70+ label list, paired with the seasonal small plates
venues.whyItsHere: An intimate neo-bistro and natural wine bar in the heart of Berawa, billed as Indonesia's first natural wine bar, with a compact room of around 60 covers and a curated list of 70+ natural-wine labels from small producers alongside ingredient-focused food. An evenings-only spot for food and wine lovers.
venues.notFor: Large groups or anyone after a big beachfront party scene

venues.name: Motion Cafe
venues.bestFor: Gym-goers and fitness-minded travellers wanting clean, protein-forward food and healthy breakfasts.
venues.whyItsHere: A fitness-focused cafe in the Berawa area from Motion Fitness Foods, built around healthy, customizable meals, meal prep and macro-friendly plates.
venues.notFor: Diners looking for a rich, indulgent or traditional dining experience.

venues.name: Neighbourhood Food Berawa
venues.bestFor: A relaxed brunch, coffee and healthy casual daytime eating.
venues.whyItsHere: A neighbourhood cafe and coffee shop in the heart of Berawa on Jl. Pantai Berawa, serving slow breakfasts, laid-back snacks and simple wholesome dishes made from local produce; daytime hours.
venues.notFor: Not an evening dinner venue - the Berawa branch closes at 6pm.

venues.name: Neighbourhood Food Seseh
venues.bestFor: A relaxed breakfast or brunch, an easy weekend dinner, and calm coffee stops away from the crowds.
venues.whyItsHere: A local eatery and coffee shop set among the tranquil Seseh fields, serving slow breakfasts, laid-back afternoon snacks and weekend dinners with fresh, simple dishes from local produce.
venues.notFor: A high-energy party or beach-club atmosphere.

venues.name: Revolver Canggu
venues.bestFor: Coffee-led brunch after surf; a laptop-friendly morning; an easy all-day sit-down.
venues.whatToOrder: In-house-roasted specialty coffee; the Revolver Big Brekkie.
venues.whyItsHere: The Canggu outpost of Revolver, a homegrown Bali coffee brand (est. 2012) that roasts its own beans. An all-day cafe on Jl. Nelayan doing serious espresso, full brunch and heavier evening plates, turning bar-ish after dark.

venues.name: Rise and Shine Cafe
venues.bestFor: A bright, casual morning-after-the-beach breakfast or brunch, good for solo travellers, couples and small groups wanting healthy plates.
venues.whatToOrder: Breakfast burrito; super scramble; Morning Magic smoothie
venues.whyItsHere: A long-running, colourful all-day breakfast and lunch cafe on Jl. Padang Linjong in Canggu (open since 2017) serving fresh, health-conscious comfort food, coffee and juices in an open-air, al-fresco setting.
venues.notFor: A daytime breakfast-and-lunch spot rather than an evening dinner or drinks venue.

venues.name: Rize Cafe
venues.bestFor: Spice lovers wanting Indian food, a distinctive brunch, or cocktails and chai in a relaxed setting; small groups sharing dishes.
venues.whatToOrder: South Indian dosa; house masala chai
venues.whyItsHere: A Pererenan cafe serving contemporary Indian home cooking alongside brunch classics, craft cocktails and house chai, with spices ground daily and dishes rooted in family recipes.
venues.notFor: Diners after a beach-club or sunset-view scene.

venues.name: Ruko Cafe
venues.bestFor: Post-beach breakfast, brunch and coffee for solo travellers, couples and families who want fresh, organic-leaning food near Berawa.
venues.whatToOrder: Coffee on the house Indonesian blend; pancakes; avocado toast; fresh smoothies
venues.whyItsHere: An Australian-style neighbourhood cafe about 300m from Berawa beach, serving locally sourced healthy food and its own Indonesian coffee blend, with 40-50% local organic produce. A reliable daily breakfast and brunch stop.
venues.notFor: A daytime cafe rather than an evening or dinner destination.

venues.name: Sa’Mesa Canggu
venues.bestFor: Groups and social diners up for a shared, festive Italian feast; couples and solo travellers happy to sit with strangers.
venues.whatToOrder: The Experience Dinner set menu (the only format); house-made limoncello.
venues.whyItsHere: A one-of-a-kind communal Italian dinner: everyone shares one long table for a multi-course, family-style set menu inspired by an Italian grandmother's table. Dinner only, one seating, book ahead — it's an event as much as a meal.
venues.notFor: A quick a-la-carte bite or a quiet private table — it's a communal, fixed-menu experience with one long shared table.

venues.name: Santanera
venues.bestFor: Date night; group dinners built around shared plates; a special evening with cocktails on the rooftop.
venues.whatToOrder: Baby squid with black garlic mole; dry-aged duck.
venues.whyItsHere: A stylish Latin American dining room and rooftop bar on Jl. Tanah Barak, blending Latin flavours with European technique and local ingredients across a sharing-focused menu. Contemporary artwork, a big seated room and a late-night bar make it a full evening out.

venues.name: Seseh General Store
venues.bestFor: A morning coffee and pastry, a casual sandwich-and-salad lunch, and a laid-back neighbourhood cafe stop after the beach.
venues.whatToOrder: Milk-based coffee (flat white); sandwiches and salads
venues.whyItsHere: An Aussie-owned corner cafe bringing Melbourne coffee culture to coastal Cemagi/Seseh, doing well-made breads, sandwiches and salads a short step from the beach, with a community-hub feel.
venues.notFor: A sit-down dinner or a formal evening out.

venues.name: Shelter Restaurant
venues.bestFor: Date night, group dinners and special occasions with an atmospheric, reservation-led setting.
venues.whyItsHere: A wood-fired Middle Eastern and Mediterranean restaurant in Pererenan built inside a Balinese joglo with open, plant-filled architecture, a UK chef and a rotating calendar of BBQs and vinyl DJ nights. Reservations recommended.
venues.notFor: A walk-in quick lunch or a solo laptop session.

venues.name: SKOOL Kitchen
venues.bestFor: A special-occasion or date-night dinner with a beachside setting and drinks.
venues.whyItsHere: An open-flame kitchen perched above Batu Bolong beach (above The Lawn), where Chef Val cooks grilled meats and smoky seafood over natural wood and charcoal, paired with cocktails and a wine list; dinner only, from 5pm.
venues.notFor: Not a casual daytime or budget option - evenings only.

venues.name: The Lawn Canggu Beach Club
venues.bestFor: Sunset cocktails from a daybed; an easy day between surf sessions on Batu Bolong
venues.whyItsHere: A lifestyle beach club set directly on the black sand of Batu Bolong Beach, with an ocean-facing infinity pool, daybeds and a bar-restaurant known for sunset sessions and DJs over the Indian Ocean. A long-running Canggu spot for daytime lounging that shifts into golden-hour drinks.

venues.name: The Loft
venues.bestFor: a slow brunch after the beach; specialty coffee and a laptop; plant-based and healthy breakfast eaters
venues.fullAddress: Jl. Pantai Batu Bolong No. 50A, Canggu, Kec. Kuta Utara, Kabupaten Badung, Bali 80361
venues.whatToOrder: dragon fruit smoothie bowl; coconut pancakes; avocado on toast with poached eggs; matcha latte
venues.whyItsHere: A Bondi-inspired all-day brunch cafe on Batu Bolong with specialty coffee and a large plant-based selection. It leans to healthy breakfast staples, bowls and toasts in a relaxed Canggu setting.
venues.notFor: anyone seeking a quiet non-touristy local spot or a traditional balinese meal

venues.name: The Shady Shack
venues.bestFor: A healthy post-surf brunch; plant-based eaters; a relaxed early dinner with a rice-field view.
venues.whyItsHere: A breezy all-day vegetarian and vegan cafe overlooking the Berawa rice fields, from the team behind Betelnut. Big wholefood menu — all-day breakfast, smoothies, salads, share plates and vegan desserts — in a leafy, open setting.

venues.name: Touché Cafe & Restaurant
venues.bestFor: A stylish brunch, a coffee-and-laptop daytime stop, and a relaxed casual meal.
venues.whyItsHere: An island-chic all-day cafe near Pererenan beach with design-led interiors and an urban-leaning menu, open from early morning to late.
venues.notFor: A beach-club scene or a big group party.

venues.name: TWO FACE Canggu
venues.bestFor: Coffee-focused travellers wanting a solid morning brunch and specialty coffee in a welcoming space.
venues.whatToOrder: Specialty coffee; brunch classics
venues.whyItsHere: A specialty coffee and brunch spot on Jl. Munduk Catu in Canggu, focused on well-made coffee, creative brunch dishes and a relaxed community atmosphere.
venues.notFor: Late-night diners; the kitchen is a daytime brunch operation.

venues.name: Udara Bali
venues.bestFor: Wellness- and health-minded diners wanting calm, organic food; a quiet, restorative meal near the beach.
venues.whyItsHere: Organic Ocean, the plant-forward restaurant at the Udara wellness retreat in Seseh, focused on healthy, community- and earth-conscious cooking, open daily to guests and visitors.
venues.notFor: A lively nightlife scene or a heavy meat-and-cocktails night out.

venues.name: Ulekan Berawa
venues.bestFor: A relaxed dinner of traditional Indonesian food; groups sharing classic dishes; an accessible introduction to the country's cuisine.
venues.whatToOrder: Beef Rendang; Sate Ayam; Gado Gado.
venues.whyItsHere: A sit-down Indonesian restaurant in Berawa celebrating dishes from across the archipelago — hand-ground spices, no MSG or palm oil, ingredients sourced from small-scale farmers and fishermen. A pretty, fairy-lit garden setting.

venues.name: Woods Bali
venues.bestFor: A cosy, atmospheric dinner; date night; groups who want a relaxed evening with live music.
venues.whyItsHere: A rustic Pererenan restaurant built entirely from reclaimed wood, serving a Mediterranean menu in a warm, greenery-filled chalet setting. Cafe by day, dinner room by night, with live jazz and vinyl evenings.

venues.name: YUKI Canggu
venues.bestFor: A stylish date night, special occasion or group dinner with cocktails.
venues.whyItsHere: A modern Japanese izakaya steps from Batu Bolong beach serving fusion sushi, wood-fired specialties and signature cocktails in a stylish yet laid-back room; bookings are essential.
venues.notFor: Not a budget or walk-in-anytime spot - reservations recommended.

venues.name: Gong Restaurant
venues.bestFor: couples and small parties wanting a stylish traditional-Balinese dinner; a special evening centred on local cuisine
venues.whatToOrder: Balinese Rijsttafel; slow-cooked bebek betutu; pork ribs in local spice paste; lawar; nasi campur
venues.whyItsHere: Signature Indonesian and Balinese restaurant of Kayumanis Sanur (Jl. Tirta Akasa No. 28), offering an intimate resort setting focused on traditional dishes. Open daily 7am–10pm.
venues.notFor: a quick casual bite or a large, loud group

venues.name: Boy'N'Cow
venues.bestFor: A carnivore-focused dinner out or a special occasion for serious steak and Wagyu lovers who want cocktails alongside.
venues.whatToOrder: dry-aged steak; wagyu; porterhouse or tomahawk cuts
venues.whyItsHere: A premium steakhouse and cocktail lounge on Seminyak's Eat Street (Jl. Raya Kerobokan), open since 2017, serving dry-aged cuts cured on site for at least 30 days plus Australian, US and Japanese Wagyu including A5 grades; the industrial meat-boutique room pairs share-style steaks with a mezzanine speakeasy bar known for barrel-aged cocktails and single malts.

venues.name: Corner House Bali
venues.bestFor: brunch after the beach; an easy first-night dinner; a relaxed group meal of familiar Western food
venues.whatToOrder: modern Australian breakfast; burgers; char-grilled steak; fish and chips; wood-fired pizza
venues.whyItsHere: All-day cafe and bistro on Seminyak's "Eat Street," serving Western/modern-Australian classics from breakfast and brunch through burgers, char-grilled steaks and pizza. Casual, generous, colonial-house setting on the corner of Oberoi Road.
venues.notFor: travellers specifically after Indonesian/local cuisine (menu is Western-focused)

venues.name: Kilo Kitchen Bali - Seminyak
venues.bestFor: Groups who want to share creative fusion plates and cocktails over a lively dinner.
venues.whatToOrder: squid ink rice; grilled fish; creative fusion plates
venues.whyItsHere: The first overseas outpost of Singapore's Kilo, on Jl. Drupadi in Seminyak, serving Asian-fusion sharing plates — squid-ink rice with soft-shell crab, beef-tongue tacos, Japanese ceviche — in a tropical-industrial space of concrete, wood and glass.

venues.name: Kynd Community
venues.bestFor: Vegan and vegetarian brunch; an iconic smoothie-bowl stop; a lighter reset day
venues.whatToOrder: Smoothie bowls; pancakes; burger; berry smoothies
venues.whyItsHere: Seminyak's strongest plant-forward brunch — a big, walk-in cafe known for smoothie bowls and colourful comfort food.
venues.notFor: A quiet, low-key table — it runs busy and social

venues.name: La Casetta Bali
venues.bestFor: a cozy, unhurried Italian dinner; a relaxed breakfast or lunch; a laid-back family or couples meal
venues.whatToOrder: daily-baked cornetti and bread; fresh pasta; homemade pizza; classic cocktails
venues.whyItsHere: A home-style Italian restaurant serving daily-baked bread and cornetti, fresh pasta and wood-fired pizza in a rustic, cozy setting, running from breakfast through lunch to dinner.
venues.notFor: travellers wanting a beachfront or main-Seminyak-strip location (it sits inland in Umalas/Bumbak)

venues.name: Ling-Ling's Bali
venues.bestFor: Groups and dates wanting a shared-plates dinner that rolls into cocktails and music.
venues.whatToOrder: sushi; bao; dumplings
venues.whyItsHere: A lively Asian-fusion restaurant and cocktail spot on Jl. Petitenget in Seminyak, leaning Japanese and Korean with sushi rolls, Korean tacos, bao buns and pork ribs, plus a DJ and a long drinks list after dark.

venues.name: Mamasan Bali
venues.bestFor: A dressed-up dinner or cocktails for couples and groups who want polished Asian flavours in an atmospheric setting.
venues.whatToOrder: dim sum; curries; noodles and roti
venues.whyItsHere: Chef Will Meyrick's long-running pan-Asian dining room in Seminyak, plating elevated Southeast Asian street food — from Penang to Bangkok to Indonesian rendang — inside a dramatic 1920s Shanghai-style room of dark timber, green banquettes and chandeliers, with an upstairs supper-club bar.

venues.name: MOONLITE KITCHEN & BAR
venues.subarea: Seminyak beachside (Anantara, Jl. Abimanyu / Dhyana Pura)
venues.bestFor: couples wanting a rooftop dinner with a view; travellers after cocktails at golden hour; a lively night out with music
venues.whatToOrder: Balinese crispy duck; Bali baby pork ribs; Black Snow signature cocktail; Black Mamba signature cocktail
venues.whyItsHere: Rooftop bar and restaurant on top of Anantara Seminyak Bali Resort, serving modern Southeast Asian dishes with ocean views. Nightly live bands and DJ sets give it an energetic, dressed-up evening feel.
venues.notFor: anyone wanting a quiet, low-key meal (live music/DJ most nights)

venues.name: Natys Restaurant Seminyak
venues.bestFor: groups sharing grilled seafood and ribs; visitors wanting all-day/late dining on Eat Street; cocktail-and-shisha evenings
venues.whatToOrder: fresh grilled fish from the fishmonger display; Indonesian grilled chicken; giant grilled ribs; fresh Balinese-fruit juices; cocktails
venues.whyItsHere: All-day restaurant on Jl. Kayu Aya (Oberoi/Eat Street), Seminyak, serving Indonesian, seafood and European dishes; a front fishmonger display lets guests pick fresh fish for the grill. Open breakfast through late night, with cocktails and shisha.
venues.notFor: diners seeking a quiet rice-field or off-strip setting

venues.name: Naughty Nuri's Warung Seminyak
venues.bestFor: a group here for smoky ribs and martinis; a casual, no-fuss dinner; an easy meal soon after landing
venues.whatToOrder: BBQ pork ribs; martinis; Thursday sushi night
venues.whyItsHere: The Seminyak-area (Batu Belig) branch, opened in 2016, of the Ubud institution — known for Balinese-style barbecued pork ribs and its potent martinis, in a casual warung format.
venues.notFor: diners wanting a refined, quiet dining room, or those avoiding pork (ribs are the draw)

venues.name: Seasalt
venues.bestFor: date night special; sunset drinks view; special occasion
venues.whatToOrder: seafood dishes; Sunday brunch items; cocktails
venues.whyItsHere: Beachfront seafood-led fine dining inside Alila, strongest for coastal date nights and brunch splurges.

venues.name: Sisterfields
venues.bestFor: Brunch and good coffee between Seminyak shopping — a lively, dependable all-day cafe.
venues.whatToOrder: avocado toast; pancakes; salads and sandwiches
venues.whyItsHere: A long-running Australian-style cafe on Jl. Kayu Cendana in central Seminyak, opposite Seminyak Village, serving an all-day brunch menu and specialty coffee in a bright, tiled minimalist space — signature plates include truffle scrambled eggs, bacon benedict, smashed avocado and acai bowls.

venues.name: Think Pink Salon & Nails Bali
venues.bestFor: nails, lash and beauty appointments; friends going together for a pampering session; travellers wanting a wide gel/BIAB and nail-art menu
venues.whatToOrder: Manicure & pedicure (nail art)
venues.whyItsHere: A long-running nail, hair and skin salon offering manicures, pedicures, BIAB and nail art with an extensive polish range, plus hair and skin services. Rooms include a private space for a treatment while watching Netflix.
venues.notFor: walk-ins expecting immediate slots (advance booking is advised); those wanting massage/spa-focused wellness rather than salon services

venues.name: Warung Nia Balinese Food & Pork Ribs
venues.bestFor: A relaxed, well-priced local lunch or dinner for travellers wanting authentic Balinese pork ribs without the fine-dining setting.
venues.whatToOrder: pork ribs; chicken satay; Balinese rijsttafel
venues.whyItsHere: A casual Balinese warung in Kayu Aya Square on Jl. Kayu Aya (the Oberoi strip), best known for slow-cooked, spice-marinated pork ribs sold by weight with a range of chilli levels, plus babi guling, satay and rijsttafel.

venues.name: Alchemy
venues.bestFor: Plant-based and gluten-free diners; a post-yoga breakfast; a customisable healthy start; off-peak laptop time
venues.whatToOrder: Breakfast Bar; avocado toast; banana-taro pancakes; Champion Breakfast; açaí bowl
venues.whyItsHere: Ubud's clearest dietary-first breakfast — a fully plant-based, gluten-free menu with a customisable raw breakfast bar and transparent pricing.
venues.notFor: A conventional sit-down brunch, or families needing kids' facilities

venues.name: Bali Bohemia
venues.bestFor: Health-conscious travellers wanting a plant-forward breakfast, bakery pastries, or a relaxed dinner with live music in a colourful, creative setting.
venues.whyItsHere: A boutique-hotel restaurant and bakery next to the Sacred Monkey Forest Sanctuary, built around fresh, wholesome East-Mediterranean-inspired cooking with a plant-forward focus, plus a standalone bakery arm and live-music evenings.
venues.notFor: Guests wanting a strictly local Balinese/Indonesian menu or a quiet, music-free dinner.

venues.name: Bali Buda Ubud
venues.bestFor: Health-conscious travelers, longer-stay visitors, and mixed groups wanting a big menu with vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free options (including Bali's only gluten-free pizza base) in one place.
venues.whatToOrder: Cassava pancakes; gluten-free pizza; nasi campur or gado gado.
venues.whyItsHere: Long-running Bali wholefoods institution (operating in Ubud since 1994) combining an organic grocery/bakery with an all-day restaurant covering raw vegan, Italian, and traditional Indonesian dishes.
venues.notFor: Travelers seeking an intimate fine-dining or special-occasion setting — it's a casual, functional health-food restaurant and shop.

venues.name: Cafe Wayan & Bakery
venues.bestFor: Groups or families wanting an easy, established garden restaurant with a broad menu and a well-known dessert to share.
venues.whatToOrder: Death by Chocolate cake (the cafe's signature, decades-old dessert)
venues.whyItsHere: Established in 1986 on Monkey Forest Road, a long-running Ubud institution with a bakery out front and a rice-paddy-view garden behind, serving a wide Indonesian/Thai/Italian menu plus a Sunday-evening traditional Balinese buffet.
venues.notFor: Diners seeking a quiet, intimate table (it's a large, high-turnover tourist institution).

venues.name: Casa Luna
venues.bestFor: Travellers wanting a genuine, long-established Balinese dining experience in the centre of Ubud, from breakfast through dinner.
venues.whatToOrder: Lime tart (a limited daily-morning batch that regularly sells out by mid-afternoon); Balinese-style paella; smoked duck feast for two
venues.whyItsHere: Ubud's most enduring restaurant, opened in 1992 by food writer Janet DeNeefe on Jalan Raya Ubud; the menu moves between authentic Balinese cuisine (nasi campur, ceremonial curries, slow-cooked spice-paste dishes) and a modern Mediterranean side, with pastries from the sister Honeymoon Bakery.
venues.notFor: Very budget-conscious travellers looking for street-warung prices (a well-known, mid-range destination restaurant).

venues.name: CasCades
venues.bestFor: Couples or small groups wanting a scenic, sit-down valley-view meal with a curated Indonesian tasting format.
venues.whatToOrder: Royal Rijsttafel tasting plate; Wild Mushroom & Truffle Risotto
venues.whyItsHere: The gourmet restaurant of Viceroy Bali (also branded as part of CasCades Suites), perched atop the Valley of the Kings about five minutes' drive from central Ubud, pairing panoramic jungle-valley views with a Balinese Rijsttafel tasting menu and Western dishes built on produce from its own organic gardens.
venues.notFor: Travellers without their own transport looking for a walk-in meal in central Ubud, or budget diners.

venues.name: Dicarik Warung
venues.subarea: Jl. Kajeng (rice-field walk, north of Ubud centre)
venues.bestFor: travelers wanting a hands-on Balinese cooking class amid rice fields, or pre-ordered betutu bebek
venues.fullAddress: Jl. Kajeng, Subak Juwuk Manis, Ubud 80571
venues.whatToOrder: Betutu Bebek (slow-roasted duck, pre-order required); hands-on Balinese cooking class (7 dishes + 2 drinks)
venues.whyItsHere: A rice-field-walk warung on Jl. Kajeng (Subak Juwuk Manis) north of central Ubud, run by a mother-daughter team, known primarily for its hands-on Balinese cooking class (garden spice ID, 7 dishes + 2 drinks) alongside a la carte Balinese food such as betutu bebek.
venues.notFor: those wanting walk-in a-la-carte dining without any advance notice

venues.name: Donna Ubud
venues.bestFor: Groups or couples wanting a stylish dinner with cocktails and a lively nightlife atmosphere in central Ubud.
venues.whatToOrder: Seafood/Peruvian paella; 24k Gold Wagyu steak with truffle mash; grilled octopus
venues.whyItsHere: A restaurant, lounge and rooftop bar in the middle of Jalan Monkey Forest blending Mediterranean and Latin American cuisine, open daily from late morning until late, with a Friday-night club upstairs and shisha available after 4pm.
venues.notFor: Guests wanting a quiet, early, low-key dinner (the venue turns into a nightclub on Friday nights).

venues.name: Fair Warung Balé
venues.bestFor: Travellers wanting an easy, good-value Indonesian meal that also funds a local healthcare program; casual lunch or early dinner.
venues.whatToOrder: Chicken satay; tuna fillet; cashew chicken
venues.whyItsHere: A social-enterprise warung on Jalan Sriwedari (Taman Kaja) run by the Fair Future Foundation/Bali Sari Foundation, where restaurant proceeds fund free medical consultations for the local community; serves Indonesian, Asian-fusion and vegetarian dishes daily.
venues.notFor: Diners seeking upscale fine dining or a view/sunset setting (a simple, ten-table warung).

venues.name: Hujan Locale
venues.bestFor: Travellers wanting an elevated, chef-driven take on Indonesian regional cooking for a dinner or shared meal.
venues.whatToOrder: Nasi Bakar Babi (slow-cooked pork belly with black-nut rice in banana leaf, grilled over coconut husk, served with black bean broth)
venues.whyItsHere: A modern Indonesian restaurant in Ubud Center by chef Will Meyrick, housed in a stylishly renovated two-story building (bar downstairs, dining room with temple and street views upstairs), reworking street-food flavours from Java, Sumatra, Sulawesi and Bali with modern presentation and locally sourced ingredients.
venues.notFor: Diners wanting a cheap, no-frills traditional warung experience.

venues.name: Ibu Rai
venues.bestFor: Travellers wanting an easy, centrally located sit-down meal that mixes Indonesian classics with familiar European comfort dishes.
venues.whatToOrder: Nasi goreng; honey ginger prawns
venues.whyItsHere: A long-running restaurant and gallery on Jalan Monkey Forest founded by the family of Ibu Rai (born 1925, who ran a food stall near Ubud Palace); opened as a restaurant in 1992 and now serves a fusion of Indonesian specialties and European dishes in a central, art-filled setting.
venues.notFor: Guests wanting a quiet, private table away from Ubud's busiest tourist strip.

venues.name: Karsa Cafe
venues.bestFor: Travelers finishing the Campuhan Ridge Walk who want a calm, scenic break with healthy food; quiet daytime pause surrounded by rice fields.
venues.whatToOrder: Seasonal fruit smoothie (changes with what's in season); vegetarian local Indonesian dishes.
venues.whyItsHere: A rice-paddy and jungle-valley hideaway café along the Campuhan Ridge Walk, serving organic meals, seasonal fruit smoothies, and gourmet coffee with unobstructed green views.
venues.notFor: Large groups or late-night plans — it's a small daytime venue (8am-6pm) reached by a walk down into the valley.

venues.name: Locavore NXT
venues.bestFor: Food-focused travellers wanting a multi-course, reservation-based tasting-menu experience celebrating Indonesian farmers, fishers and producers.
venues.whyItsHere: Opened December 2023 in Lodtunduh as the more experimental, ingredient-driven sibling of Ubud's famous fine-dining restaurant Locavore, led by chefs Eelke Plasmeijer and Ray Adriansyah; a 30-seat tasting-menu space using only local Indonesian ingredients with modern European technique, ranked among Asia's 50 Best Restaurants.
venues.notFor: Casual walk-in diners, budget travellers, or anyone wanting a quick, low-key meal (advance-reservation, multi-course tasting format only).

venues.name: Milk & Madu Ubud
venues.bestFor: Families with children; mixed groups; a central meeting point; anyone who wants a fixed breakfast bundle
venues.whatToOrder: Ricotta cakes; smashed avocado; cinnamon-bun French toast; the 110K breakfast set
venues.whyItsHere: The easiest family and group brunch in Ubud — an all-day cafe with a clear 110K breakfast set, a kids menu and group bookings for up to 20.
venues.notFor: A quiet weekend brunch — there is live music on Saturday and Sunday, 11:00–14:00

venues.name: Mozaic
venues.bestFor: special-occasion diners wanting French-Balinese tasting-menu fine dining in a garden setting
venues.whatToOrder: 6-course seasonal tasting menu; 8-course tasting menu; vegetarian botanical tasting menu
venues.whyItsHere: A long-running fine-dining anchor in Kedewatan founded by Chef Chris Salans and now led in the kitchen by head chef and co-owner Blake Thornley, built around French technique and Indonesian/Balinese ingredients, with a multi-course tasting menu plus a separate live-kitchen Chef's Table.
venues.notFor: travelers on a tight daily budget or wanting a quick casual meal

venues.name: Room4Dessert
venues.bestFor: dessert-focused special occasions and pastry enthusiasts wanting a multi-course tasting experience
venues.whatToOrder: Full dessert tasting menu (with pairings); Spiritless tasting menu (non-alcoholic pairing)
venues.whyItsHere: Chef Will Goldfarb's dessert-only tasting-menu restaurant in Kedewatan, set inside a large botanical garden tied to the dessert recipes themselves — a globally recognized pastry-chef concept rather than a conventional dinner spot.
venues.notFor: travelers wanting a full savory dinner or a budget meal

venues.name: Seeds of Life
venues.bestFor: Raw-food and vegan-focused travelers, and wellness/yoga-oriented visitors wanting a calm plant-based meal in central Ubud.
venues.whatToOrder: Raw lasagne (zucchini, Napoli tomato sauce, cashew-nut sauce); cauliflower gnocchi with cashew pesto; SOL Bowl; choc-berry smoothie bowl.
venues.whyItsHere: A 100% raw & vegan restaurant and Taoist tonic bar in Ubud Center (on Jalan Goutama, operating since 2014), built around raw-food dishes made from fresh, locally grown produce, with Ashtanga yoga classes upstairs.
venues.notFor: Travelers wanting hearty non-vegan or cooked-comfort-food options.

venues.name: Seniman Coffee Studio
venues.bestFor: Remote workers and digital nomads wanting a serious specialty-coffee spot with room to sit and work; coffee enthusiasts wanting origin-focused brews.
venues.whatToOrder: Espresso or filter brews from Indonesian single origins; detox juice (beetroot, lime, ginger, carrot); sate or soto ayam.
venues.whyItsHere: A well-known Ubud specialty coffee roaster and café near Ubud Palace, treating coffee as a craft with sourced-and-roasted Indonesian origin beans, multiple brew methods, and a food menu spanning Indonesian and Western dishes.
venues.notFor: Travelers wanting fast grab-and-go service — service here leans toward a slower, deliberate pace.

venues.name: Suka Espresso Ubud
venues.bestFor: A first brunch in Ubud; coffee lovers; solo diners and couples; a short off-peak laptop session
venues.fullAddress: Jl. Raya Pengosekan Ubud No.108, Ubud, Gianyar 80571
venues.whatToOrder: Truffle scramble; Big Brekky; eggs benedict; chilli tofu scramble; smoothie bowls
venues.whyItsHere: The safest all-round brunch in Ubud — Australian-style breakfast and specialty coffee from 7:30, with 60K breakfast specials running until 3pm and clear vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free labels.
venues.notFor: A quiet, half-empty table at peak brunch — it gets busy

venues.name: The Elephant
venues.bestFor: vegetarian and vegan travelers wanting an all-day scenic meal overlooking Campuhan Ridge
venues.whatToOrder: vegetarian bolognese pasta; veggie burger; lumpia
venues.whyItsHere: An open-air, 100% vegetarian/vegan restaurant on the Campuhan side of Ubud (Jl. Raya Sanggingan) built around an elevated terrace overlooking Campuhan Ridge, open all day from breakfast through dinner, with an organic/local/slow-food sourcing philosophy.
venues.notFor: diners specifically wanting meat or seafood dishes

venues.name: Warung Biah Biah
venues.bestFor: authentic Balinese food; budget local dining; nasi campur; a casual central Ubud meal
venues.fullAddress: Jl. Goutama Sel. No.13, Ubud, Kecamatan Ubud, Kabupaten Gianyar, Bali 80571, Indonesia
venues.whatToOrder: nasi campur; balinese tapas; mie goreng; soto ayam; nasi goreng
venues.whyItsHere: A long-running home-style Balinese warung open since 2002, serving authentic nasi campur and local classics at warung prices. Known for its house chili sauce and a spread of Balinese sides.
venues.notFor: diners wanting a quiet or upscale setting; groups needing lots of space during busy peak hours

venues.name: Watercress Ubud
venues.bestFor: An early, composed breakfast; couples who want to talk; small early meetings; groups who book ahead
venues.whatToOrder: Smashed avocado; truffled mushroom scramble; eggs benny; Turkish eggs; Benny Brunch set
venues.whyItsHere: The most polished early breakfast in Ubud — open from 7am with an unusually clear, current menu; breakfast service runs to 11:30.
venues.notFor: All-afternoon brunch — breakfast stops at 11:30

venues.name: Zest Ubud
venues.bestFor: Vegan diners; a social brunch with friends; an atmosphere-led daytime meal; a date that does not need silence
venues.fullAddress: Jl. Penestanan No.7, Sayan, Ubud, Gianyar 80571
venues.whatToOrder: Chickpea omelet; Royal Breakfast; avocado toast; waffles; smoothie bowls (all vegan, most available gluten-free)
venues.whyItsHere: The strongest creative vegan brunch in Ubud — an all-plant-based menu of substantial savoury dishes in a treetop Penestanan setting.
venues.notFor: A quiet, low-key table — it leans social, with music

venues.name: Alchemy Uluwatu
venues.bestFor: A daytime health-focused meal or casual dinner for vegans, raw-food eaters and anyone wanting to build their own salad after the Bukit beaches.
venues.whatToOrder: bowls; jackfruit salad; pizza/kombucha
venues.whyItsHere: The Bukit outpost of Bali's long-running plant-based restaurant Alchemy (original in Ubud), serving raw and gently cooked vegan food built around a build-your-own salad bar, smoothie bowls and a waffle-and-ice-cream bar, in a large ranch-style al-fresco space with a permaculture garden and an evening bonfire near Bingin/Pecatu.

venues.name: BGS Uluwatu
venues.bestFor: brunch after surf and quiet work session
venues.whatToOrder: almond latte; light bites; bakery
venues.whyItsHere: An Uluwatu surf shop and coffee bar near the Suluban/Uluwatu beach entrance, known for its coffee and laid-back surf-community vibe.

venues.name: Gooseberry French Restaurant | Uluwatu
venues.bestFor: An unhurried, dressed-up meal away from the crowds — from an all-day breakfast to a leisurely dinner for couples or small groups.
venues.whatToOrder: onglet classique; confit de canard; barramundi la persillade
venues.whyItsHere: A modern French restaurant on a garden lane near Bingin Beach in Pecatu, serving casual French cooking with a barbecue influence — steak tartare, foie gras, grilled cuts and soufflé pancakes — across breakfast, lunch and dinner; set around a poolside terrace with tropical interiors rather than a beach view, with a screen-free policy at the table.

venues.name: Papi Sapi
venues.bestFor: Meat lovers wanting a relaxed evening of pick-your-cut grilling, dinner and drinks in the Uluwatu area.
venues.whatToOrder: premium grills; wagyu rib eye; picanha
venues.whyItsHere: A fire-driven neighbourhood grill on Jl. Labuansait in Pecatu (the Bukit), where guests pick cuts of meat, seafood and vegetables from a display chiller — priced by weight per 100 grams — to be cooked over live flames; open evenings only, with live music on Thursdays and DJs on other nights.

venues.name: The Cashew Tree
venues.bestFor: post-surf brunch near bingin; healthy plant-forward meal; laid-back live-music night; casual group hang
venues.fullAddress: Jl. Pantai Bingin No.9, Pecatu, Kec. Kuta Sel., Kabupaten Badung, Bali 80361
venues.whatToOrder: the soul bowl; corn fritters; quesadillas; fresh juices and smoothies; vegan cakes and desserts
venues.whyItsHere: A relaxed, vegetarian-leaning cafe set in a leafy open-air space near Bingin, serving wholesome all-day plates and hosting weekly live-music nights. It doubles as a social hub with regular Thursday music and a fitness/yoga community on site.
venues.notFor: anyone wanting a formal, fine-dining or meat-forward dinner

venues.name: The Warung at Alila Villas Uluwatu
venues.bestFor: date night special; special occasion; family early dinner
venues.whatToOrder: megibung banquet; bebek goreng; red bean rendang
venues.whyItsHere: In-resort Indonesian dining room with polished service and strong special-occasion appeal.

venues.name: Ulu Fishmarket
venues.bestFor: Relaxed group dinners and casual seafood lunches for surfers and travellers staying around Bingin and Padang Padang.
venues.whatToOrder: sashimi trio; tuna tataki; miso ramen
venues.whyItsHere: A casual open-air seafood restaurant on Jl. Labuansait in Pecatu, near Bingin, modelled on Maui's Paia Fish Market and serving a Hawaiian-Japanese-Peruvian fusion of fresh catch, poke, sushi, ceviche and fish burgers in a tropical garden setting.

venues.name: Ulu Garden
venues.bestFor: Groups, couples and families wanting a relaxed evening of garden dining with live music, and a rare kid-friendly option in the Bukit.
venues.whatToOrder: miso gnocchi; ulu fresh rolls; rice bowl
venues.whyItsHere: A tropical-garden restaurant tucked off Jalan Pantai Padang-Padang in Pecatu, part of the Ulu Tribe hospitality complex, serving a contemporary take on Indonesian cooking with modern-European and healthy-cafe touches; known for its lush, string-lit garden with nightly live music and Balinese dance and blessing rituals.

venues.name: WAATU
venues.bestFor: Dinner for couples or groups who want live-fire, produce-driven sharing plates with a clifftop ocean view; reservations recommended.
venues.whatToOrder: fire-cooked local produce; yakitori
venues.whyItsHere: A clifftop yakitori and open-flame grill restaurant at The Ungasan Clifftop Resort in Uluwatu, led by Australian chef James Viles, where everything is cooked over coals with no gas or electricity; the share-style menu runs from woodfired mortadella flatbread and yakitori skewers to pork-and-scallop fried rice, with lawn and cliff-edge views over the Indian Ocean.

venues.name: YUKI Uluwatu
venues.bestFor: date night special; group dinner share; special occasion
venues.whatToOrder: wagyu sando; tuna tartare; charcoal grill plates
venues.whyItsHere: A design-forward modern Japanese izakaya in Uluwatu, serving sushi and grilled plates with sake and cocktails, liveliest as an evening dinner-and-drinks spot.

venues.name: ZALI Uluwatu
venues.bestFor: group dinner share; family early dinner; date night special
venues.whatToOrder: samke harra
venues.whyItsHere: Modern Lebanese/Mediterranean sharing restaurant with family-friendly all-day positioning.
